description: Companion repository for social norms, social influence, peer effects, reciprocity, cooperation, herd behavior, diffusion, norm change, and collective action.

articles.title: What Are Social Norms?
articles.summary: A foundational article on informal expectations and behavior.

articles.title: Social Influence and Human Behavior
articles.summary: How other people shape action and judgment.

articles.title: Descriptive Norms and Injunctive Norms
articles.summary: What people do versus what people approve.

articles.title: Conformity, Compliance, and Obedience
articles.summary: Classic mechanisms of social influence.

articles.title: Social Proof and Behavioral Imitation
articles.summary: How visible behavior becomes a cue for action.

articles.title: Norms as Informal Institutions
articles.summary: Norms as rules outside formal law.

articles.title: How Social Norms Emerge
articles.summary: The formation of shared expectations.

articles.title: How Norms Stabilize Behavior
articles.summary: How sanctions, approval, and repetition hold behavior in place.

articles.title: Sanctions, Rewards, and Social Approval
articles.summary: Social consequences and norm maintenance.

articles.title: Reputation and Social Monitoring
articles.summary: Visibility, credibility, and trust.

articles.title: Identity and Group-Based Norms
articles.summary: Group membership and behavioral expectation.

articles.title: Peer Effects
articles.summary: How peers influence decisions and outcomes.

articles.title: Social Comparison
articles.summary: How comparison shapes satisfaction, goals, and behavior.

articles.title: Reciprocity and Cooperation
articles.summary: Mutual response and collective behavior.

articles.title: Herd Behavior
articles.summary: Following others under uncertainty.

articles.title: Informational Cascades
articles.summary: When behavior spreads through observed decisions.

articles.title: Diffusion of Behavior Through Networks
articles.summary: How behavior spreads across social structures.

articles.title: Changing Social Norms
articles.summary: How norm change happens and why it is difficult.

articles.title: Norm-Based Messaging
articles.summary: Designing communications around perceived norms.

articles.title: Correcting Misperceived Norms
articles.summary: When false beliefs about others sustain behavior.

articles.title: Social Norms and Sustainability
articles.summary: Visible participation, conservation, consumption, and collective action.

responsible_use: Educational and research-scaffolding examples only; not operational guidance without validation and ethical review.
